source: DT-agent-managed-codewiki-gc accepted by user after post-commit GC clarification

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Tracked `.codewiki` artifacts must not be purged until an archive commit containing revive context exists.
GC must write restore evidence that names archive commit/tree, removed paths, and restore commands before deleting tracked artifacts from the hot tree.
Agent skills must make post-commit GC review mandatory at close/publication boundaries and require block/defer evidence when skipped.
A public source-owned GC path must support dry-run, scoped purge, tracked artifact gates, and ignored runtime cleanup without manual file deletion.

evidence_mapping.evidence: User accepted GC-001 after clarifying that GC should happen after commit so revive context remains in Git.
User accepted GC-002 requiring `.codewiki` maintenance in skill output/flow contracts.
User accepted GC-003 requiring source-owned GC use case/tool path.

agent_assessment: Post-commit GC is the correct safety boundary. Pre-commit tracked deletion is unsafe because the commit would lack the full artifacts needed for revival. The simpler runtime-only cleanup can happen independently, but tracked build/validation purge needs archive proof and restore ledger.
summary: Accepted decision to enforce post-commit CodeWiki garbage collection as agent-owned flow

diff_table.current_state: GC policy exists in knowledge/config/graph views, but sprint-close flow does not execute cleanup after immutable archival proof. Agents can close tasks/sprints and leave pass validations/builds/runtime handoffs piling up, or risk deleting tracked evidence before it is safely archived in Git.
diff_table.desired_state: Agent flow must include an explicit post-commit GC gate after task-close/sprint-close/publication archive commit exists. The archive commit stores full revive context. GC then runs against that exact commit SHA/tree, writes a compact restore ledger/commands, purges eligible cold/pass/runtime artifacts, and commits the deletion/ledger as a separate GC commit or blocks with reason.
diff_table.rationale: Git should be the cold archive. Deleting tracked CodeWiki artifacts before the archival commit is unsafe. Keeping them in the latest tree forever is noisy. A two-step archive commit -> GC commit preserves restoration while keeping hot `.codewiki` small.
diff_table.risk: High if deletion lacks archive commit proof or if GC amends/squashes away the archive commit. Medium if only ignored runtime files are cleaned. Gate must require immutable Git proof and forbid purging tracked builds/validations before archive commit exists.
alternatives: Single commit containing restore ledger but not full artifacts; rejected because the deleted artifacts would not exist in that commit tree.
Pre-commit GC; rejected because it can remove revive context before Git archives it.

diff_table.current_state: Skills mention validation/task-close but do not make GC an end-of-flow invariant. Main/implementation/planning/validation skills can complete work without any trash-removal check.
diff_table.desired_state: Skill contracts must require post-commit GC review during task-close/sprint-close/publication: agent owns `.codewiki` maintenance, cannot leave eligible cold/pass/runtime artifacts hot unless blocked/deferred with evidence.
diff_table.rationale: If GC is only a tool, agents may forget it. The rule belongs in the skill flow and output contract so every agent sees it.
diff_table.risk: Low; prompt/process enforcement only, but should be backed by tests so prompt assets keep the rule.

diff_table.current_state: Graph computes GC classes and restore indexes, but no public application tool safely purges builds/validation/runtime handoffs with a ledger.
diff_table.desired_state: Add source-owned GC use case/tool path (`codewiki_gc` or `codewiki_task action="gc"`) that supports dry-run, scoped purge, archive-commit/tree proof, restore-ledger emission, ignored-runtime cleanup, and policy gates for tracked vs ignored artifacts.
diff_table.rationale: Manual file deletion violates the same boundary as manual roadmap edits. GC needs a safe application contract.
diff_table.risk: Medium; new mutation path must avoid deleting fail/block/current-policy reports or unarchived builds, and must not amend away the archive commit that proves deleted tracked content.

accepted_decisions.summary: Use Git as the cold archive and run tracked CodeWiki GC only after an archival commit exists.
Require a two-step archive commit then GC commit protocol for tracked builds/validations/roadmap artifacts.
Enforce GC review in agent skill flows at task-close, sprint-close, publication, and roadmap-end boundaries.
Add a source-owned safe GC application/tool path with dry-run, restore ledger, scoped purge, archive SHA/tree proof, ignored-runtime cleanup, and deletion safeguards.

assumptions: Git history remains available as the cold archive for deleted tracked CodeWiki artifacts.
The archive commit must remain reachable and must not be amended away by the GC commit.
non_goals: Do not purge fail/block/current-policy validation reports without explicit safe lifecycle rules.
Do not replace validation/task-close proof with GC ledger proof.

knowledge: Document post-commit GC protocol and archive/restore invariants in system KB.
roadmap: Create executable task(s) for GC tool/path and skill enforcement.
code: Implement source-owned GC application/tool behavior and tests.
